businessModel.text: Revenue from Pharmaceutical (Keytruda, Januvia, Gardasil, ~80%), Animal Health (Vifor integrated, ~12%), and Healthcare Services (Walgreens partnership, ~8%). High-margin proprietary drug portfolio.

recentCatalysts.text: Keytruda: global sales $25bn+ FY24; label expansions ongoing in adjuvant melanoma, bladder, gastric Caplanex acquisition (ADC platform, 2024) adds 3 oncology assets in Phase II Vifor Pharma integration (acquired 2023) — renal/vascular franchise now contributing $1.8bn Winrevair (sotatercept) approved 2024 for pulmonary arterial hypertension — c.$3bn peak potential
items: Keytruda: global sales $25bn+ FY24; label expansions ongoing in adjuvant melanoma, bladder, gastric
Caplanex acquisition (ADC platform, 2024) adds 3 oncology assets in Phase II
Vifor Pharma integration (acquired 2023) — renal/vascular franchise now contributing $1.8bn
Winrevair (sotatercept) approved 2024 for pulmonary arterial hypertension — c.$3bn peak potential

keyRisks.text: 1. Keytruda composition-of-matter patent expires 2028 — biosimilar erosion begins immediately 2. US IRA (Inflation Reduction Act) price negotiation reduces revenue on selected drugs in 2026-28 3. Clinical setback risk on pembrolizumab combination trials in 1L lung cancer 4. Vifor integration slower than planned — $700M synergies not yet realised 5. Vaccine franchise (Gardasil) faces pricing pressure in US and European Gavi markets

risks.risk: 1. Keytruda composition-of-matter patent expires 2028 — biosimilar erosion begins immediately
2. US IRA (Inflation Reduction Act) price negotiation reduces revenue on selected drugs in 2026-28
3. Clinical setback risk on pembrolizumab combination trials in 1L lung cancer
4. Vifor integration slower than planned — $700M synergies not yet realised
5. Vaccine franchise (Gardasil) faces pricing pressure in US and European Gavi markets

whoShouldOwn.text: Pharma-turnaround investor play on pipeline maturation post-Keytruda exclusivity cliff. Healthcare-portfolio diversifier with attractive dividend yield (~2.8%). NOT suitable for investors unable to hold through clinical setbacks or who require near-term revenue growth acceleration.

entryExit.text: Scale in at $108–$118 (15–20% below 52-week high). HOLD $118–$132. REDUCE above $132. Monitor: Keytruda label expansion approvals and FY26 IRA price negotiation list drugs. | BUY: Scale in at $108–$118 (15–20% below 52-week high). | REDUCE above $132.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via DYOR HQ data pipeline (Google Sheets + Yahoo Finance). Fields include price, market capitalisation, 52-week range, P/E, EPS, and volume.
sources.companyFilings: Public company filings, regulatory announcements, investor presentations, and RNS where referenced in the analysis.
